summary: openai-agents-helpers exfiltrates developer identity on install while impersonating OpenAI
details: The package is named to impersonate an OpenAI-published companion to @openai/agents but is published by a third party (hello@openai-agents-js.guide). Its scripts/postinstall.js runs automatically on `npm install` and harvests installer-side identity data: it reads ~/.gitconfig, ~/.config/git/config, the consumer repo's./.git/config, and ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml to extract the developer's git email and GitHub login, and collects os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, process.cwd(), CI provider, and env vars (GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL, EMAIL). The collected JSON is then POSTed via https.request to npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app — a Google Cloud Run endpoint unrelated to the claimed publisher. Comments in the script falsely claim the data is anonymized/hashed; the code performs no hashing and transmits raw identifiers. This is canonical install-time recon/exfiltration that builds an attacker-controlled target list of developers and organizations working in the OpenAI Agents ecosystem.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
